title: Organize Files into Category Subfolders

wrong_answer_rejection.details: Empty=0.176, Partial=0.471, Case-sensitive-bug=0.765. No wrong answer reaches 1.0. Caveat: the case-sensitive bug (misses icon.PNG because .PNG not in lowercase IMAGES set) scores 0.765, crossing the 0.6 pass_threshold. This is the precise skill the task claims to test ('case-insensitive-matching' in meta.skill_tested), so the evaluator would mark a buggy solution as PASSED. Recommend raising pass_threshold to 0.85 for this task.

anti_gaming.details: 6 hardcoded shutil.move() calls with literal filenames (photo.jpg→images/, icon.PNG→images/, readme.md→docs/, notes.txt→docs/, data.csv→other/, archive.zip→other/) scores 1.0. The checks enumerate every expected file path, making the checks-as-spec hole wide open.

style_variation.details: pathlib-based rewrite: Path.mkdir, if-elif ladder on entry.suffix.lower(), Path.glob('*'), Path.rename — scored 1.0. Checks are state-based, not code-shape-dependent.

hardening_required: Add file_count path='docs' min=2 max=2 weight=1 (currently missing — only images and other have count guards)
Add file_count path='.' min=1 max=1 weight=1 (only organize.py should remain at root after running)
Raise pass_threshold to 0.85 — ensures the case-sensitive bug (scoring 0.765) correctly FAILS the task-level verdict
suggestions: (Future) Add a hidden input file like 'diagram.JPG' or 'FOO.TXT' that's materialized at setup time but NOT enumerated in checks. Validate via file_count only. This would catch both hardcoding AND the case-sensitive bug simultaneously. Requires evaluator support for 'hidden_input' field.
Consider adding a .JPEG file (uppercase) to input to give the case-insensitive matching more coverage.
meta_note: Same Test B meta-note as mb-001/mb-002. Structural issue, not a task-specific flaw.
reasoning: Well-specified prompt with explicit exclusion rules (script not moved, pre-existing dirs not moved). Multi-step logic (dir creation + classification + filesystem ops). Reference solution handles the spec correctly. ACCEPT with hardenings; the docs file_count and root file_count additions close most of the hardcoding gap without requiring held-out inputs.
